definition: Private-sector Treasury absorption requirements of $2.7-3.3T annually are a dominant structural driver of term premium. QT runoff ~$300B/year plus net new issuance ~$2.0-2.6T plus declining foreign official demand (~$100-200B/year net selling) equals 5-6x QE-era absorption levels. Observable in deteriorating auction metrics.
evidence: Foreign official demand declining ~$100-200B/year net selling
Arithmetic not contested by any agent
open_questions: What is the price elasticity of private-sector Treasury demand?
Will foreign private demand (e.g., Japanese lifers) offset official selling?
At what term premium level does absorption become unconstrained?
